raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's affordable housing crisis continues to worsen as unsubsidized affordable housing is rapidly disappearing from the market.",
    "Low-income families are increasingly unable to find affordable housing due to an imbalance between housing production and demand.",
    "SB 225 aims to assist in the acquisition and rehabilitation of existing unsubsidized housing to convert it into long-term affordable housing.",
    "The Community Anti-Displacement and Preservation Program (CAPP) is designed to prevent displacement and homelessness by stabilizing low-income families.",
    "State resources are necessary to scale the work of community-based organizations and developers in acquiring properties for affordable housing.",
    "More than half of the affordable rental housing in the U.S. is provided without subsidy in the private market.",
    "In California, nearly 1.1 million units of affordable rental housing are available without subsidy.",
    "CAPP provides a new tool for creating permanent affordability for renters at risk of being priced out of their communities.",
    "Leveraging existing housing stock can allow the state to bring affordable units online more quickly."
  ]
}
```
